entities.name: ABIOTIC STRESS
entities.description: Environmental factors that negatively impact plant growth and development

entities.name: PLANT GROWTH AND DEVELOPMENT
entities.description: The process of a plant increasing in size and maturing

entities.name: FLOWERING AND FERTILITY
entities.description: The reproductive capacity and process of plants

entities.name: OSMOLYTES
entities.description: Organic solutes accumulated by organisms to maintain osmotic balance under stress

entities.name: BACTERIA
entities.description: Single-celled microorganisms that accumulate osmolytes

entities.name: PLANTS
entities.description: Multicellular organisms that accumulate osmolytes in response to abiotic stress

entities.name: PROLINE
entities.description: An amino acid that acts as an osmolyte

entities.name: BETAINES
entities.description: Quaternary ammonium compounds that act as osmolytes

entities.name: TREHALOSE
entities.description: A sugar that acts as an osmolyte

entities.name: MANNITOL
entities.description: A polyhydric alcohol that acts as an osmolyte

entities.name: SORBITOL
entities.description: A polyhydric alcohol that acts as an osmolyte

entities.name: PINITOL
entities.description: A polyhydric alcohol that acts as an osmolyte

entities.name: CYTOPLASM
entities.description: The jelly-like substance within a cell where osmolytes accumulate

entities.name: CHLOROPLASTS
entities.description: Organelles in plant cells where osmolytes may accumulate

entities.name: OSMOTIC ADJUSTMENT
entities.description: The process of maintaining water potential to survive stress

entities.name: ABSCISIC ACID
entities.description: A phytohormone involved in stress response and signal transduction

entities.name: SIGNAL TRANSDUCTION
entities.description: The process by which a chemical or physical signal is transmitted through a cell

entities.name: STOMATAL CLOSURE
entities.description: The closing of stomata to prevent water loss, regulated by ABA

entities.name: OSMOLYTE BIOSYNTHETIC PATHWAY GENES
entities.description: Genes responsible for the synthesis of osmolytes

entities.name: NITRIC OXIDE
entities.description: A signal molecule involved in osmolyte biosynthesis

entities.name: CARBON MONOXIDE
entities.description: A signal molecule involved in osmolyte biosynthesis

entities.name: HYDROGEN SULFIDE
entities.description: A signal molecule involved in osmolyte biosynthesis

entities.name: REACTIVE OXYGEN SPECIES
entities.description: Highly reactive chemicals formed during stress that are scavenged by osmolytes

entities.name: SALT STRESS
entities.description: Abiotic stress caused by high salinity in the environment

entities.name: DROUGHT STRESS
entities.description: Abiotic stress caused by lack of water
